4
INTRODUCTION TO INSTAGRAM
WHAT IS INSTAGRAM?
At its core, Instagram is a simple social networking app designed for sharing photos and
videos. The reality: Instagram has become a cultural phenomenon. Launched in 2010,
“Insta’ has become one of the most popular apps in the world with more than 1 billion
active users. In 2012, Facebook saw the potential that Instagram had and acquired the
company for $1 Billion. The growth has not stopped.
Today, Instagram is used worldwide and is readily available on any type of device. While it
can be accessed on the web from a computer, uploading and other capabilities are only
available via mobile devices. Well over 99% of Instagram usage is done on mobile devices
or tablets
Instagram has grown to be second only to Facebook, its parent company.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 62 --

5
WHY SHOULD I USE INSTAGRAM FOR BUSINESS?
What makes Instagram unique is that it has a streamlined interface that focuses on the
visual aspect of you or your business. There is so much that you can do on Instagram, from
sharing photos, posting videos, streaming live video, and messaging with potential
customers. Instagram can help you stay on the top of people’s minds and ultimately into
their wallets.
Instagram is a place where new connections are made and current connections thrive.
With the click of a button, someone can follow you or your company forever. While growth
on Instagram isn’t an overnight process, it is something that anyone can achieve.
We will cover the various types of content you can post and how to maximize your results
with each. Additionally, we will discuss strategies for growing your followers on Instagram
and how to monetize them as well.
Let’s get to it!
You need to remember that in the world we live in, your
brand and online presence do matter. People will check
you out on Instagram before making a buying decision.

INSTAGRAM TERMINOLOGY
6
Here are a few fundamental terms you need to know in order to operate on
Instagram…
INSTAGRAM BASICS
Hide a post without deleting it.
ARCHIVE
A small excerpt of text underneath a username that gives people some more information
about a brand.
BIO
Description of text that goes underneath a post.
CAPTION
This is a section of Instagram where posts are recommended to you based on your
interests and behavior on the app.
EXPLORE PAGE
You can find this when editing a photo in the app. This editing feature allows you to
change the colors of an image to make it look more pleasing.
FILTER
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 62 --

7
This is simply someone who follows your account.
FOLLOWER
Another name for Username. Each account has a handle that is used to identify the
profile.
HANDLE
A word created by using the pound symbol (#) and having a word follow it. Hashtags are
searchable and help categorize your posts.
HASHTAG
A person or account with a large following, often in a specific niche.
INFLUENCER
This is the home screen where you can view posts from people you follow.
NEWSFEED
An alert that informs activity in your Instagram account.
NOTIFICATION
Content that is made by fans of a brand.
USER-GENERATED CONTENT
The average user spends nearly one hour a day on
Instagram. While there is considerable competition for
attention, there is limitless opportunity if you can stand out.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 62 --

8
ENGAGEMENT
A private message to a person or a group of people that is only seen by invitation.
DIRECT MESSAGE (DM)
The tagged location of a post.
GEOTAGS
Clicking the heart button to show appreciation for a post.
LIKE
A tag on an image that lets a user view a product.
PRODUCT TAG
When a user forwards a post to either all of their followers or to any particular contact or
group
SHARE
Attach an Instagram profile to a post by using the @ symbol with a username.
TAG
Realize that “DM’s” are for much more than socializing.
When done right, you can turn your Instagram messaging
into a gold mine for generating leads and closing deals.

-- 8 of 62 --

9
TYPES OF POSTS
This is a post that is similar to a GIF. Typically this is a 1-second video that plays over and
over again.
BOOMERANG
A single post that has multiple images/videos that you can swipe through to view.
CAROUSEL POST
A section of your Instagram profile where stories can be saved to.
INSTAGRAM HIGHLIGHTS
A live video that your followers can watch.
INSTAGRAM LIVE
24-hour posts that consist of several pieces of media (images and short video clips).
Stories are found by users when they click your profile photo.
INSTAGRAM STORIES
Saving posts that you want to view again.
SAVED POSTS
When using Instagram you should always be saving posts
that you like. You can use these posts for ideas for your own
content and it tells IG to show you more posts like it.

-- 9 of 62 --

10
ANALYTICAL TERMS
A formula created by Instagram that determines which posts a user will see on their
newsfeed.
ALGORITHM
Any type of reaction (likes, saves, comments, etc.) made on a post.
ENGAGEMENT
The total number of views on a post.
IMPRESSIONS
A section of Instagram where users can see statistics about their posts, profile, and
audience and see how their content is performing.
INSIGHTS
The number of unique users that see your content.
ORGANIC REACH
While I don’t know a thing about technology, I do know what
the “algorithm” wants. Learn what appeases the algorithm
Gods, and they will smile on you with free exposure.

PROFILE AND ACCOUNT SETUP
11
There are two types of Instagram accounts...
Every account on Instagram starts out as a standard personal account and then can
be optionally changed to a business account. If you already have an Instagram
account you can opt to convert your existing account for your business, or create an
entirely new account.
Here are the benefits of having a business account on Instagram:
Your business account will act as the digital storefront for you on Instagram. Let’s take
a look at how to set one up for success.
Here’s how to convert a personal account to a business account on Instagram:
BUSINESS ACCOUNT SETUP
PERSONAL These are accounts for individual people who want to
connect and share their content with friends and family.
BUSINESS There are accounts that want not only to connect with
customers but also sell a product or service (that’s you!)
Connect with customers by replying to them through comments and direct messages.
Add business information so that customers can contact you.
Promote products/services if you choose to advertise.
Get insights to see how your content performs.
Download Instagram and sign up using an email address or Facebook account.
Navigate to Settings → Account → Switch to Business Account
Add in your business information where applicable:
hours, address, phone number, email
1
2
3
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 62 --

12
There are lots of options that you can explore within Settings. Here are a few worth noting…
Notifications - Decide when you want to be updated on. Until you have significant
volume, it is recommended that you keep everything on to help with engagement.
Business - Most of this is general stuff, but there are a few interesting functionalities.
These include Frequently Asked Questions and Saved Replies (to make it easier to
respond to messages you get frequently).
Instagram Shops - This is a great feature for any business that sells
products online. It will allow you to add products for sale and also have
your products be tagged in your posts or stories. We will discuss Shops
more in a later chapter.
Privacy - Gives you a wide variety of options for many available features. Keep your
profile set on “public”. You want anyone to be able to view your posts and follow your
brand.
Account - Additional settings that include the ability to save posts, create a list of
close friends and view posts that you’ve liked.
QR Code - You can put this code anywhere such as on a sign or your
business cards. People can scan by taking a picture to follow you on
Instagram. Try taking a photo of Grant’s code with your phone to see
how it works.
For further detailed information on how to set up or change an account to a business
account you can use the resources below.
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Instagram Intro and Tour (10xresource.com/ig1)
• Instagram Setup Checklist (10xdownload.com/ig4)
• Getting Started on Instagram (10xresource.com/ig3)

DEFINING YOUR INSTAGRAM GOALS
CREATE YOUR BRAND STRATEGY
Instagram is all about showing your audience that your
company is more than just a logo. If you haven’t defined
your company brand, you’ll never build a loyal following.
When creating any marketing strategy, you need to be crystal clear on your objectives.
Establishing your Instagram goals will help you determine everything from the
aesthetics of your profile to the type of content you produce.
Given that Instagram is a visual platform, it is important to have a clear understanding
of how you want to present yourself and your company.
A simple exercise that you can do is to create a list of adjectives that embody your
brand. Here is an example of adjectives that could be used to describe Grant and his
personal brand...
You should focus on objectives that are in line with your overall business goals and
strategies.
AUDIENCE AND STRATEGY
Examples goals that your Instagram strategy can be centered around:
Build brand awareness
Showcase your products/services
Establish authority in your industry
Build a community of brand advocates
Increase sales, online and offline
Humanize your brand
GRANT CARDONE 	IS...
Direct
Motivational
Relentless
Affluent
Charitable
Hard-working
Strong
Charismatic
Unapologetic
Experienced

14
While you want to create your own unique style, it is also worth following other Instagram
accounts that your desired audience follows.
In addition to following other profiles, it is also recommended that you follow relevant
searches (also known as “hashtags”). To do this, go to search and simply type in keywords
that you think your audience would search. Search for a few major phrases (such as
“fashion”) as well as a few specific phrases (such as “summer dresses”)
By following relevant profiles you’ll not only get ideas for
design and content, you can also make connections with
users that have an interest in your company.
Follow the Instagram accounts of all the following...
Competitors
Complimentary products
Influencers and educators
Niche websites
Industry magazines
Industry events

15
CREATING YOUR PROFILE
If you want to be followed on Instagram, it all starts with setting up an attention-grabbing
profile.
1 	Profile Picture
This is the FIRST thing people see when viewing your profile. Make sure the image you
choose is eye-catching and memorable. Smiling faces always work best. It can also be
your company’s logo or something that is on-brand, such as a product image,
spokesperson or mascot. We recommend a 200 x 200 pixels image to ensure users don't
see a blurry photo when visiting your profile.
2 	Username
You will also want to make sure that your unique username (i.e. handle name) is
recognizable and easily searchable. If your business name is already taken by another
user, then make sure your business name is the first part of your username. For example,
if a clothing brand is named John Adams then they might choose the handle
@johnadamsclothing as their username.
People want to interact with other people on social
networks, not logos or products. Even big corporations like
Nike who use their logos mostly post pictures of people.
1
2
3
4
5
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 62 --

16
3 	Name
This is the name that displays above your bio and next to your profile photo. The name field
does not need to be unique, so you can choose any name you’d like. This will likely want to
use your company’s name or your name if you are building a personal brand. You may also
want to add a word or two to make your name more searchable. For example, if your
restaurant was named Frederick’s, consider naming it Frederick’s Restaurant.
4 Bio
This portion of real estate is the most overlooked as it is an extremely valuable space on
your profile. Your bio is what users will see when they click on your profile photo or
name. You only have a max limit of 150 characters so make sure you cover who you are
and why someone should follow you (with a hint of personality!) It helps to use emojis
(try emojipedia.com). Last, but not least, be sure to give a call to action for the link that
appears directly below.
5 	Website (Link)
You’re allowed only one link in your bio, so make it count! In fact, Instagram does not allow
links anywhere else in the app other than in Stories. You will usually want to link to whatever
is most likely to convert to a lead or a sale. That may be a squeeze page, a product page, or a
sales video. Oftentimes, businesses will refer to this link on individual posts by stating “check
out the link in my bio”.
If your company has a large number of links you’d like to promote, like Grant does, the best
way to bypass this link issue is by utilizing a “link-in-profile” tool. These tools allow you to
create simple landing pages where users can access several different pages, products and
services of your company. Grant uses a popular service called Linktree
(10xresource.com/linktree) which is pictured here.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.

• Page - Connect your Facebook page to increase cross-promotion

• Contact Options - Add email and/or phone to allow people to reach you

POSTING AND CONTENT
18
So you’ve got an attention-grabbing username chosen, a rockstar bio written, and an
eye-catching profile photo uploaded... now what do you do? You post. Let’s take a look
at the types of posts that you can make.
There are several types of content you can post to your feed. The feed contains content
that is displayed on your Instagram account. We’ll first dive into this type of content
first since this is the first place that most users begin posting on.
Here are a few reasons you’d want to post on your feed:
POST AND CONTENT TYPES
INSTAGRAM FEED CONTENT
If you want the photo or video to be permanently visible for others to view.
If you want your Instagram feed to follow a particular design or theme.
If you are just starting out on Instagram or want to grow your following,
the main feed is where people can find you.
1.
2.
3.
Images
Want to make a quick post? Snap a photo... it’s easy as 1,2,3. You can also upload a
previously taken photo from your phone or computer. After adding an image you’ll be
able to use the many cool effects that made Instagram famous.
That said, keep in mind that image posts don’t always need to be photos. You can
create any number of various graphics as seen in the examples below. While
dimensions can vary, it is usually better not to post wide photos. They don’t fully
optimize the space when someone is scrolling on a mobile phone.
Random sample of Grant’s image posts

-- 18 of 62 --

19
Videos
Video content is very valuable for both viewers and content creators. Videos reduce
the amount of reading and writing effort needed by both parties. On Instagram,
standard video content is limited to 60 seconds unless you are posting an IGTV
(long-form video).
Carousels
A carousel is simply multiple images or videos combined in one post. They are posted
in a format that allows a viewer to swipe through each image, hence the ‘carousel’
name. This can be used creatively and allows a content creator to get more mileage
out of a single post. Only one caption/description is used for all the videos and images
included.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 62 --

20
Reels are no different than normal video posts, except for
they have more features and can be seen by more people.
I highly recommend that you focus heavily on Reels.
Reels
Inspired by TikTok, Reels are short 90 second long video clips that you can share to
your stories and explore feed. There is a tab on your profile for Reels. With Reels, you
can be very creative and set your video to music. Instagram also has a variety of
features including speed control and AR effects so that you can let your imagination
run wild.
Live
Engage and interact with your followers in real-time using live streaming video.
With Instagram Live, you can showcase the unedited, unfiltered version of yourself.
Go Live. End the broadcast — save your live stream. Live is an excellent way to boost
engagement and start to build a real relationship with the people who follow you.
Story Posts
Stories allow you to chain together images and videos in order to share a visual
narrative. Each video or image in your Instagram Story can be up to 15 seconds long,
and you can include up to 100 pieces of content in each Story. They are a great way to
stay relevant and top-of-mind with your audience.
One important thing to realize about Stories is that they disappear after 24 hours.
Even with a limited shelf life, Stories can generate a high number of views because
they are listed at the top of everyone’s home feed.
INSTAGRAM STORIES
Stories are a very popular Instagram feature. They are similar to carousels and other forms
of content, but they are unique in how they are accessed and how long they are posted.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 62 --

21
While Stories are often used to capture a timeline of in-the-moment clips or photos,
you can also include pre-created images or videos. Additionally, Stories provide a large
number of interactive features such as polls and quizzes which can be added to each
image or video. They are great for posting time-sensitive information on your story to
help communicate urgency for offers and promotions.
You should think about your Story Highlights as if they were the menu for your
Instagram profile. When used correctly, they can greatly increase the number of
people that you convert from Instagram to become a lead or make a purchase.
One of the benefits of Stories is they appear top and center.
One of the most important features in Stories is that they allow you
to post links to your website. This is done in what is called a “Link
Sticker”. Note that to unlock this feature you must have 10,000 or
more followers.
Story Highlights
Instagram Story Highlights are a special kind of Story that you select to permanently
feature at the top of your page, directly under your profile. This is a great way to
showcase some of your most important or memorable moments, as well as link to
your websites and products.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

22
CONTENT CREATION
You’ll notice features on IG are similar to other networks.
For example, Stories are also on Facebook and Reels are
like Shorts on Youtube. Repurpose your content!
We’ve covered the types of posts on Instagram, so now we will take a look at how you
can MAKE your content. There are many different ways you can record, shoot, or create
the content for Instagram, so we will only be covering the most recommended
strategies in this manual.
Before you make content, you should first be clear on the purpose for creating it.
Some questions that you should ask yourself when making a post include:
When you take into consideration the purpose of your post it will help the entire
content creation process. Your caption and visuals will be more cohesive, which will
help you have a stronger impact on your Instagram posts.
Instagram started as a photo-sharing app, so the bulk of the content that you will see
on the app will be photos. There is no right or wrong way to take a photo, but there are
a few tips that will help you take better photographs. Let’s cover two key photography
components: composition and lighting.
ESTABLISHING THE PURPOSE OF YOUR CONTENT
TAKING QUALITY PHOTOS AND VIDEO
Do I want to tell a story?
Am I making someone laugh?
Do I want to motivate people?
Is this educational or informative?
Am I sharing an experience?
Should I show vulnerability?
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 62 --

23
Smartphones these days come with a wide variety of
professional features and settings, including a grid for aligning
your subject. Take a quick minute to learn your camera.
Composition
When composition is done well, it allows the viewers to focus on the subject of the photo
while expressing a story and emotion. This is where the ‘Rule Of Thirds’ comes in. This
widely accepted design principle requires you to imagine the image divided into thirds,
both horizontally and vertically. The key is to place the subject at any of the 4 points in
which the lines intersect.
The benefit? Following the Rule of Thirds creates a more pleasing image that helps guide
the viewer’s eye. Remember that users on Instagram are scrolling, so you need to do
anything you can to make your image grab attention.
Lighting
Ideally you’ll want to use natural light when taking photos. Artificial lighting can
sometimes add a tint to your photos. If inside, it is usually best to sit or stand near a
window.
The time of day which you shoot can also greatly affect the lighting. For example, dusk
and dawn are generally great times for natural lighting, or when it’s cloudy. Shooting
under direct sunlight will be too harsh for photos.
This photo would feel way different with the barn centered
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 62 --

24
Many people use Instagram without sound. Be sure to
integrate captions and text on your video to capture the
attention of people who cannot hear your video’s audio.
While natural lighting is ideal, it is not always realistic,
especially if you’re trying to capture a lot of photos and
videos. The simplest and most inexpensive solution is a ring
light. For less than $40 you can purchase a kit that includes
a 12” light, tripod and remote. (10xresource.com/ig4)
Shooting Video
Video content can help immensely in your content strategy, allowing you to tell in-depth
stories, while also being more educational or entertaining. Here are some essential tips
when shooting a video for Instagram.
It is worth noting that nearly any smartphone will produce high enough quality video to
use on Instagram. While you may consider investing in a more expensive camera at
some point, it is likely not worth it initially.
Other than lighting, the only other possible investment
would be in a quality microphone. Having clear sound is
important, especially if you are filming in an environment
with background noise. Depending on your phone, you can
generally find a lavalier mic (the type that pins to your shirt)
that will get the job done.
For your main feed, you can shoot video vertically or horizontally.
For Stories, it is highly recommended to shoot your video vertically.
Movement is great, but avoid being shaky or wobbly.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

FILTERS AND EFFECTS
Photo and Video Filters
When adding a standard post, either photo or video, you will be given options to adjust
the image. Instagram provides a wide range of one-click “filters” that change the overall
look and feel of your media. Here are a few examples…
If the quality of your images is very important to you, such as for fashion or photography
influencers, you can also customize photos even further using Instagram’s edit feature.
Editing allows you to control things like contrast, saturation and color.
Reel and Story Features
When you create Reels or Stories you will see a number of different features that are not
included on standard posts…
This makes it easy to add your favorite tune, or better yet, a song
that will add to the theme of your video. Simply click to search a
wide library, which includes many popular songs across a large
number of genres.
This allows you to choose from a large number of effects that make
your photo or video come to life. From simple sparkles to alien
takeovers, you can get very creative with this feature.
Stickers allow you to add a variety of effects over your image or
video. This includes interactive options such as polls, sliders, quizzes
and countdown timers. In this section you will also find link stickers,
which allow you to send users to your website.

26
CREATING GRAPHIC POSTS
While they may seem a bit cheesy, you should use the
sticker features on Reels and Stories. They give people a
reason to stop scrolling and interact with your content.
This allows you to doodle on your image or video. You can choose
from a large number of colors and writing styles.
This is used to add simple text in a wide range of font styles.
Graphic Post Types
Typically, graphics do not receive as much engagement from viewers as photo or video,
however there are a few exceptions. One that Grant uses often is quotes.
Consistently posting photos of Grants and his “Grantisms” has played a very large part in
establishing his personal brand. While using original photos and quotes is beneficial for
obvious reasons, keep in mind that you can also use stock photos and quotes from other
people.
Examples of some famous Grant one-liners
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 62 --

27
Another type of graphical post to consider are “memes”.
A meme is a comedic image, video, text, etc. that is copied
(with variations) and spread rapidly on the internet. People
embrace comedy on social media and that is why memes are
popular on not only Instagram, but social media in general.
There are many other graphical styles that you can explore including infographics, lists and
illustrations. In general, when posting graphics keep them informative, entertaining, and
clean in design.
Video and Image Editing
While Instagram does offer a variety of tools and features natively to help you improve
your content, there are many times when you will want to do more. For example, you
may want to post professional videos or stylish graphics like the ones you see above.
While you can always hire a professional, the reality is that your social media posts may
not produce revenue immediately. If you are on a budget you might consider using
services such as Fiverr (10xresource.com/fiverr) to hire inexpensive labor. Alternatively,
there are tools that allow you to edit images and videos yourself.
If you don’t know how to edit video, and you don’t have a team member
who does, then you’ll likely want to get an online editing software. Promo
makes it easy to make videos, and even provides video clips you can use at
no extra cost. (10xresource.com/promo)
If you do not have a designer on staff, then consider using an online image
editor. Software services such as Canva are specifically designed for social
networks like Facebook. They are easy to use and automatically size your
images for you. (10xresource.com/canva)

28
Regardless of how you decide to make content, remember that you can always get better
with time. The biggest thing that slows up new creators is trying to be perfect.
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Instagram Example Swipe File (10xdownload.com/ig8)
• Instagram Posts and Features Tutorial (10xresource.com/ig2)
Captions are the text component of any post. They can be up to 2,200 characters in length
and can include emojis, hashtags and user tags. Captions should be attention-grabbing
and easy to read and follow.
The goal when writing captions is to help increase the engagement on your post. The
more engaging your post is, the more people Instagram will display your post to.
POSTING STRATEGIES
CRAFTING POST CAPTIONS
Consider running contests to increase engagement
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 62 --

29
At first you might be scared to ask for participation out of
fear you won’t get replies. Don’t be. While you may start slow,
you’ll never get activity unless you put yourself out there.
I recommended adding between 3 to 10 hashtags per post.
You can use up to 30, but adding too many hashtags can
look spammy and actually diminish the overall value.
What makes a great Instagram caption? A compelling caption is one that entertains the
reader or makes them take action. Length does not matter with Instagram captions, but do
be aware that people move through Instagram at a fast pace so you’ll want to write the most
important part of your caption at the beginning.
Some key tips when writing captions:
Hashtags are any word or phrase that is included in a caption that follows the # symbol. On
Instagram, the main purpose of using a hashtag is to make your content more discoverable.
Users can type a hashtag into the search bar in Instagram and discover a collection of
content that is related to that word. Think of it like typing a keyword into a search engine.
Hashtags are also clickable. Any hashtagged phrase on Instagram turns into a link which
leads users to a feed of other content with the same hashtag.
USING HASHTAGS
Know your brand voice.
Know your audience.
Have fun with emojis.
Be educational, informative, humorous, or shocking.
Insight conversation by asking your audience a question.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 62 --

30
A great way to leverage hashtags is to create a branded hashtag that you promote regularly.
List your special hashtag in your bio and encourage your followers to use it. You can view
users who have used your hashtag and reshare their content to your page or Instagram
story. This will help create relationships between you and your followers and establish brand
advocates.
Tagging (not to be confused with hashtagging) is where you tag the account of another
user. When you tag someone it creates a link to their profile and also makes it more likely
for their followers to see your post. User tagging can be done in two ways…
When you tag a user, your photo or video will actually show up on a tab of the tagged
content on that person’s page. The user will also get a separate notification that is different
from the standard list of notifications on Instagram.
Example: 	@grantcardone 	@elenacardone 	@jarrodglandt 	@cardonecapital
ADDING USER TAGS
Add their username after the @ symbol in a caption.	1
Adding a tag in one of your photo or video post	2
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 62 --

31
Remember that it isn’t about putting out content that you
think people want. It’s about putting out content that
represents who you are, and finding the people who resonate.
Your alt text can simply consist of a handful of key terms that explain what is in your
image. These can be words such as the location, the item of clothing you're wearing,
the activity you are doing, the theme of your profile and more.
To add alt text to your post, follow the normal posting steps, but after you edit your
photo, go to ‘Advanced Settings’. Next click ‘Write Alt Text’ under the ‘Accessibility’
section. Write your alt text and then you're done!
Alt text is short for ‘alternative text’. Technically, the alt text for an image functions as a
text description when the image doesn’t load due to reasons such as poor internet
connectivity.
That said, alt text can be much more valuable. This feature is often overlooked, but it
actually helps the Instagram algorithm know what your post is about. This will help
increase the likelihood that your post shows up in the ‘explore feed’ and will help your
content reach the right people.
USING ALT TEXT
ADDITIONAL RESOURCE:

-- 31 of 62 --

32
The algorithm is what decides what content is displayed
and what is not. The more people interact with your stuff,
the more likely it is to be displayed. It’s that simple.
ENGAGEMENT FEATURES
Remember that Instagram is a social media platform. You want to be familiar with all
the ways that users can interact with your posts and content. Furthermore, you will
want to understand these features so that you can engage with other user’s content.
We’ll discuss specific strategies for engagement in a later chapter, here are the actual
features that make Instagram social...
Like
“Liking” a post on Instagram is the simplest and quickest way to show another
account that you care. You can like any type of post (images, videos, Stories,
Reels, IGTV, etc) as well as individual comments.
Comment
If a user has an opinion on a post, a comment is the natural next level of
engagement after a like. Submitting a comment is as easy as typing and
Instagram provides an emoji button to help spice it up.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 62 --

33
Share
By clicking the share icon a user is able to quickly send the post with one or more
specific of their Instagram contacts. Additionally, they can create a group of
people to share with or add the post as a Story.
Save
The newest interactive feature on Instagram, the save function allows people to
easily come back to your post later. If your post is something of high value,
suggest that people save it in your post caption.
Pinning Comments
Pinning a comment is a simple action that allows you to highlight a comment
you deem important. Pinned comments will stay at the top of your comment
section of your post. This is also a good strategy to use when updating a post.
Story Reactions
When viewing Instagram stories you can react to a user’s story in two ways: one,
you can type a message on a user’s story, and two, you can react via an emoji.
Story reactions will show up as a direct message in the user’s inbox.
Direct Messages
One of the most important and frequently used features on Instagram is direct
messaging (often referred to as DM). One of the unique aspects of Instagram is
that anyone can message anyone, assuming their account is not set to private.
We will further cover direct messaging strategies in further detail later in the manual.
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 62 --

34
I’ve said it many times, but I’ll say it again: quantity is more
important than quality. Your content doesn’t have to be
perfect, but it does need to be consistent to stay top of mind.
CONTENT MANAGEMENT
There is no set limit to how many times you can post on Instagram. Many accounts tend
to stick to a range of 1-3 posts per day on their account. Needless to say, @grantcardone
likes to post more frequently than most.
Ideally, how frequently you should post can be determined through testing. Generally
speaking, the more likes, shares, saves, and comments you get, the more your account
will gain momentum. That said, if you do start seeing your engagement and reach start
to go down, you can re-evaluate. Each brand and industry will have a different
frequency of posts.
One of the best things you can do for your brand is to update your Story
daily. Remember, Stories get great engagement and disappear every
24 hours. Most importantly, Stories can include links to your website, which
can pay big dividends.
POSTING FREQUENCY
Instagram’s algorithm relies largely on user
interaction when determining what posts
should appear on a person’s news feed.
As you can see in the chart provided by
Sprout Social (10xresource.com/sproutsocial),
there are certain days and times that are
generally more active than others.
POST TIMING
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 62 --

35
While understanding general trends is helpful, the key is to post your content when
most of your followers are active online. When determining what time you should
post you should consider these two points:
To know when your followers are most active you can view your ‘Insights’ from your
profiles and click ‘Your Audience’. Scroll to the bottom of this page and you will see a
section called ‘Most Active Time’. Your follower activity will be shown in the form of a
bar graph here and you will be able to see the analytics by the day and hour.
For the second question, you will need to hypothesize when your brand is relevant to
your followers. For example, if your account is for a restaurant, then posting a photo
of a sandwich during lunch would make sense.
As Grant likes to say, ‘If you’re failing to plan,
you’re planning to fail”. While making a
calendar can be a daunting task at first, it
will help you stay consistent with posting
content on Instagram. Consistency is your
#1 key to growth.
CONTENT CALENDAR
When do you have the highest number
of active followers on Instagram?
When is your brand most relevant
for your followers?
We’ve provided a weekly content planner
to make it easy
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 62 --

36
A secret to winning on IG is to post a good mix of pre-
planned content along with organic posts as life happens.
This allows you to be consistent, while staying human.
It is recommended that you plan your content week by week, but if you’d like, you can
schedule multiple weeks in advance. The best approach is to think about what you want
to achieve overall, then add specific content to reach those goals. For example, you may
decide that you want to do one image post a day, or that you want to do one promotional
post in a given week.
Planning a schedule doesn’t mean that you should not stay flexible. You can always
adjust and make changes to what and when you post. A perfect example is to capitalize
on current events, which has been hugely successful for Grant.
We use Sprout Social as we’ve found it to be a great tool to manage all of our
social platforms. It is especially helpful for managing team collaboration,
creating automated posting calendars, and producing multi-channel statistics.

GROWTH AND MONETIZATION
37
INSIGHTS & REPORTING
Now that you have learned how to create and manage content, we will cover how to
monitor your account performance. Periodically you will need to analyze your content
and make adjustments so that your account is meeting or exceeding its key
performance indicators.
NOTE: Instagram insights and analytics are only available for professional and business
accounts, so be sure to switch your personal account to a business account in settings.
Instagram has built-in metrics in the backend of your Instagram account called Insights.
Insights can be accessed through your Instagram home page. You can also see your
metrics in Facebook’s Creator Studio or third-party metric tools. Your insights are broken
down into the following sections below.
INSIGHTS FEATURES
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 62 --

38
Insights Overview:
• Accounts Reached
• Content Interactions
• Total Followers
Content You Shared:
• Feed Posts
• Stories
• Reels
• IGTV
• LIve
INSIGHTS OVERVIEW
Accounts Reached
This insights section shows you how many
non-followers and followers each type of content you
post is reaching. You can use this section to help you
decide which type of content you need to focus on so
that your account can grow. For example, if Reels are
helping you reach a large portion of non-followers,
then you may want to produce more Reels to reach a
new audience.
Content Interactions
This section breaks down the way your audience is
engaging with each type of content that you have
posted - Feed Posts, Stories, IGTV, Reels, and Live
Streams. Metrics that you will see in this section
include likes, comments, shares, saves, and replies.
Total Followers
This is a very important section that shows the demographics of your followers. The
following details of your followers are displayed:
Follower Growth (Overall, Follows, and Unfollows)
Top Locations (Cities and Countries)
Age Range (All, Men, and Women)
Gender (Men, Women)
Most Active Times (Hours and Days)
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 62 --

39
You can monitor your follower growth overtime to help you adjust your content strategy.
If you see a spike in follower growth then you can create more content that is similar to the
post that caused the rise. In addition, you can tailor your content to your audience so that
you receive the maximum amount of engagement.
Each type of content you post has several metrics that can be viewed. You are able to
view the performance of content as far as two years back.
To help optimize your content, you’ll want to focus on metrics that are receiving high
numbers. If you see a particular Instagram story that is receiving a high number of
impressions that means that this type of content resonates with your audience.
Reach is an important metric to consider when discussing feed posts and IGTV
posts. The higher number of people reached means that the Instagram algorithm is
deeming your content as engaging and is showing it to more people.
As you have seen, there are MANY Instagram metrics. Tracking all of them may not
be the best use of your time. This is why it’s important to have a brand strategy so
that you establish which metrics to track.
CONTENT YOU SHARED

40
For many, a high number of impressions and reach will matter if the goal is to grow your
audience. You’ll also want to constantly monitor the audience section of your insights to
see what type of content is causing a rise in followers or any unfollows.
For others who are aiming for sales and have instagram shopping enabled, product
button clicks or product views will be metrics to consider. These stats can be viewed by
clicking on the individual ‘Insights’ option on a post with tagged products.
If your goal is to drive sales and/or opt-ins off of Instagram, then the metrics for ‘Website
Taps’ from your profile and for ‘Link Clicks’ on stories will be valuable to track.
Remember, you must have at least 10,000 followers to add a link to your Instagram Story.
In your social media strategy, one of your first goals should be to increase the
engagement of your content. This can include looking at metrics such as like,
comments, shares, saves and views. You’ll want to track these and focus on the
content that produces that most engagement so that your day-to-day updates and
posts gain attention.

41
Make it a ritual to review your numbers at least once a week.
If you’re like me, you’ll obsess over it constantly. Like with
anything, what you focus on most is going to grow.
FOLLOWERS AND ENGAGEMENT
How often should you check your insights? There is no set answer but it is
recommended to run either weekly or monthly reports on all your social media, not
just instagram.
It should be noted that many content creators and business owners track the
performance of some posts hourly. This is because much of the engagement for posts
comes through when a post is first published.
Additionally, we recommend doing a yearly report so you can view your progress over
a long period of time.
If you are only posting content, you are missing out on the vital social aspect of the
platform. To use Instagram effectively you need to drive on BOTH sides of the street.
Being an engaged user on Instagram will not only increase the visibility of your brand,
but most importantly helps you create real relationships with your followers.
2 types of content to engage with:
• Anyone who comments on your posts
• Posts by your followers or influencers
2 primary ways you’ll want to engage:
• Liking comments or posts
• Commenting on comments or posts
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.

-- 41 of 62 --

42
People may not believe it, but I personally reply to comments
on Instagram every single day. It has won me lots of loyal fans
and also led to relationships with serious A-players.
You’ll want to do this for a few reasons. First, when you like or comment on a post, it is
possible that the poster will take notice. This increases the likelihood that someone will like
your content back which in turn will help raise the engagement of your posts.
Additionally, when you comment it puts your profile link all across Instagram, allowing
new followers to find you.
It is absolutely essential that when you get comments you like and reply. This shows
your followers that you are actively listening to what they are saying. You shouldn’t wait
however, to receive a comment to engage with another account. You should take the
initiative to comment on another user’s post to help strengthen your relationship with
them.
Before we dig into how to proactively engage your audience, you should identify who
they are.
From a content perspective, you should always be aware of the demographics of your
audience. In the ‘Insights’ section of your account you will be able to find your audience
demographics statistics. The three key insights Instagram gives about your audience are:
This will help you tailor your posts so that it resonates with your followers. It can also help
you realize if you are not reaching a certain audience you want to be reaching.
AUDIENCE TARGETING
Location 	Gender 	Age
Keep in mind that the more you use Instagram as a user,
the more likely Instagram will be to have your content
appear in user’s feeds. Appease the all mighty algorithm.

From an engagement standpoint, you will largely be able to target your audience using
two of the strategies we discussed earlier: hashtag searches and industry influencers.
Identify 10 relevant hashtag searches - These should be of varying search
volume. For example, your list could include #marketing, #onlinemarketing,
#onlinemarketingtips
Identify 10 industry leaders or influencers - These can be any individuals or
brands that you want to be associated with and/or your audience follows.
ENGAGEMENT
METHOD
PREPARATION
1
2
Search for each of the 10 hashtags by typing in the appropriate #hashtag into
the search bar. Then click on each of the top 10 trending posts for that hashtag,
leave a comment and like.
Go to the profile of each of the 10 top industry accounts. Like and leave a
comment on one post for each account.
DAILY ACTIVITY
1
2
Make sure you always leave comments that provide something of value. Ideally,
you will leave a quick compliment and then contribute to the conversation in a
meaningful way.
When leaving comments on influencer posts be sure not to be redundant. For
example, don’t over compliment a user to the point that it seems like flattery.
From time to time leave user tags (@) in the comments. You can either tag a
user you think will find the post valuable or the actual creator, to grab their
attention.
COMMENTING TIPS

You’ll notice that the end result comes out to 110 comments a day. While this is certainly

Instagram is a great place to build community; however, sometimes you might want to
showcase personal content or exclusive content (such as a special offer) to a select
audience.
The ‘Close Friends List’ is a feature that allows you to do just that. You can share your
Instagram story or content to a select group of followers that you have curated. No one
but you can see your close friends list. Once a follower is added to your list they will be
able to see the tag on the top of their story.
You will get more followers from your ideal target audience.
Your profile will begin to be recommended on their profile.
You can start to build relationships with industry influencers.
CLOSE FRIENDS LIST
The more relevant connections you have, the more you will get recommended.

45
There is no shame in paying to get your posts seen.
We live in a “pay to play” society, and your money is
less expensive than your time.
Here are a few ways a business can use the close friends list feature:
Working with influencers
If your business has an influencer partnership program, you can share exclusive
announcements and offers with them.
Update Brand Ambassadors
Share exclusive offers and updates with your most loyal fans. This allows you to
strengthen your relationship with your best fans by giving them priority and
importance.
Contact with clients
If you are a business that is client-based then you may want to share special
content with followers who you do business with. This is a great way to offer tips
and anything relevant to them that may not be applicable to all of your audience.

PROMOTING POSTS
Ever wonder how you’re going to get attention with so many other profiles fighting for
the same eyeballs? While the strategies taught in this manual will help, there is only
one way to guarantee your growth: paid promotions.
It is highly recommended that you only promote posts that have
already proven to perform. Otherwise you will waste money.

46
Facebook (Instagram’s parent company) does provide advanced advertising options in
the Facebook Ad manager. If you prefer to keep it simple, you can promote your posts
from directly inside your Instagram account. Here are the basic steps…
* You will always choose the “More Profile Visits” goal to get more followers
When it comes to advertising, you only need 2 things to be successful: a winning
message and a winning audience. Since you are only going to promote posts that are
proven to perform, the biggest factor will be defining the ideal audience.
Go to your main profile
Click the post you would like to promote. Again, be sure it is one that is
proven to resonate with your audience.
Click on Promote under the image or video
Select your goal for the promotion…
a. More Profile Visits* (good for growing followers)
b. More Website Visits (good for opt-ins or sales)
c. More Messages (good for service businesses)
Define the audience for your promoted post. You have 2 options to
choose from…
a. Let Instagram do Automatic (less expensive)
b. Custom targeting (higher quality, see below)
Set your budget and duration. You can always start off small (as low as
$5 per day)
Launch your promotion
Keep an eye on performance in Insights
TARGETING AND AUDIENCES

Primary Inbox
All messages go into your primary but you can move them to your “general”
inbox if they are not as important. You will receive notifications sent to your
primary inbox if you have your DM notifications turned on.
General Inbox
Messages that are in your general inbox are messages that you won’t
receive a notification for. These messages are typically less important and
are to be read when you have time.

Sending a direct message (“DM”) can go a long way for building relationships and
getting deals done on Instagram. Messaging is an especially valuable way to get
prospects off of the social network and onto a website or phone call. You can use
messages to achieve the following...
With a business account your DM inbox is divided into three categories:
Set Up Sales Appointments

Support Your Customers

49
As I always say, “Contacts equal contracts”. It is a
numbers game, and there has never been an easier way
to get the conversation started than IG direct messages.
You’ll want to treat direct messages as a staple engagement feature because this is
the most personal interaction you will have with another user. This feature is also
useful in resolving complaints and negative feedback that may not be fitting to
resolve in the comment section of a post.
The key is to make the conversation natural, but to keep it going down a simple
pre-determined path. While every business and product will be different, here is a
basic outline to help you create your basic script...
When you understand the power of DM and have a clear strategy, your inbox can
become a literal gold mine. Here are some quick tips for effective messenger
conversations...
MESSAGING SALES STRATEGY
Resist the temptation to make too much small talk
Control the conversation by asking questions
Always make sure that they feel heard
Don’t oversell and push away when needed
For high ticket sales, always book appointments
Message Requests
These are messages that are sent to you by people you do not follow. You
can choose to accept a message in your requests so you can read it.
3

-- 49 of 62 --

50
For this example, we’ll pretend that Bob replied to an ad about increasing his sales
team’s performance…
STEP 1 • Bonding and rapport
→ Ask a question based on their profile. Make it quick!
STEP 2 • Find out their goals
→ Learn what they are trying to achieve. Relate with them!
STEP 3 • Discover their challenges
→ Find out all their pain points. Be sure to drill deep!!!
STEP 4 • Book appointment
→ Give them a quick compliment and schedule a meeting.
STEP 5 • Grab Phone and Email
EXAMPLE CONVERSATION
Hi, I am interested in increasing my sales. Can you help?
Hey Bob, how’s it going in Ohio!?!
I’m great, how are you doing?
Yeah, those guys are nuts, lol.
5 right now, but I would love to hire more
Sounds like you’re already doing pretty well, how much
would you like your team to do every month if we could
help you expand? Round numbers.
Got it. I hear that a lot, lol.
Living the dream :)
So tell me Bob, how many people are on your sales team?
That is awesome! Glad to hear you’re expanding.
Is it mostly inbound or outbound?
Right now almost all outbound, would love to change that too, lol.
I have a cousin that lives in Columbus. I went to a
Buckeyes game with him and it was CRAZY, lol :)

I’d like to get to $200,000 a month, but we’ve
been stuck at $100k for a long time

Want to do a phone call?
I can totally relate to that. I remember that feeling

Overall performance of a few of the guys
could be better. I could probably do a

Seems like everytime I hire someone, someone else quits.

So what has been stopping you from

Oh man, that is the worst.

52
While having conversations like the one above is certainly ideal for high ticket sales, there
are situations where it may not be worth having that type of discussion. For example, if
you sell low ticket products or you are just too busy to stay top of your DM inbox. For
these situations, you may want to consider Instagram automation.
Automation is a feature that requires using a third-party tool. While it sounds technical, it
is something that you can do by following some tips.
Select a keyword phrase
(in this example, the word “yes”)
Set up an automated message with desired
messaging
→ You can collect email and phone leads, set
appointments, etc.
Connect your message to the keyword phrase
Tell your followers to send the keyword phrase
to your DM
The automated message takes care of the rest!
→ You can set up customized messages to ask
questions, etc
INSTAGRAM AUTOMATION
That’s great, look forward to it
bob@yadayada.com and 555.555.5555
3:00 is perfect, let’s Zoom if that works.
Sound like a plan?
Great, see you Thursday at 3!
Let’s plan for 30 minutes so I can learn a little
more about your business and share the details
on how we may be able to help.
Cool, and in case I need to reach out, what’s
your phone and best email? I won’t reach
out unless it’s important.

-- 52 of 62 --

53
Don’t be afraid to sell your products on Instagram. In fact,
shopping posts can actually be very engaging when they
showcase your products being used in interactive ways.
People use Instagram for connecting with people, sharing their experiences...and even
shopping! In fact, many people discover new products on a daily basis.
Instagram Shopping provides a set of features to showcase your products across
Instagram. People can shop directly from your photos and videos, no matter where they
are in the app. Businesses can showcase their product catalog and even create a
customizable storefront that allows users to shop directly on business pages.
Brands can also be featured on the Instagram shop tab from the main navigation menu.
Users will be shown products they are most likely to buy based on their previous
engagement on Instagram.
ADDITIONAL RESOURCES:
• Get Started with ManyChat (10xresource.com/manychat)
• Instagram Automation Training (10xresource.com/ig7)
INSTAGRAM SHOPPING
If you’re interested in automating your Instagram messaging for sales and
lead generation, then ManyChat (10xresource.com/manychat) is a great
option. Using ManyChat, anyone can create an automated “chatbot” with
zero coding or experience needed. They even provide the training to get
you started.

-- 54 of 62 --

TIPS, TRICKS AND HACKS
55
The further you get into Instagram, the more you realize it is an entire ecosystem. There
are over a billion sets of eyeballs on the app every month, which means there is a lot of
money and power to go around.
The Instagram economy is one that is largely traded in influence and what is referred to
as “shoutouts”. You may have seen these before. Shoutouts generally consist of a popular
influencer with a large number of followers mentioning and/or displaying a product,
company or most frequently, another Instagram account.
#1 PROFILE SHOUTOUTS
For $1,000,000 Kim Kardashian might post your product (for a few hours)
There are 3 basic types of shoutouts that you can receive…
Organic Shoutout
This is when a user mentions your profile (tags you with @) without requesting
payment or a returned shoutout. This is usually when a user likes a product or is
somehow giving thanks.
1
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 55 of 62 --

56
Paid Shoutouts
The cost is completely negotiable and can range from $20 to Kardashian money.
Sometimes influencers will take free products instead of cash. Any paid post
should include #ad.
2
Reciprocal Shoutouts
This is when two accounts agree to tag each other in a simple cross promotion.
This is sometimes referred to as “Shout for Shout” (S4S). These usually require a
good number of followers.
3
So how do you score shoutouts? There are 3 ways you can go about doing it...
OPTION #1 	PROFESSIONAL AGENCIES
There are many reputable firms that represent large influencers. While the pros can get
you premium placements, they do come with a hefty budget.
OPTION #2 	ONLINE MARKETPLACES
There are several sites that sell shoutouts such as Shoutcart or Shoutout Experts. While this
is certainly a lot less expensive than option 1, it is also a lot more hit-and-miss. Some will
work, while others won’t.
OPTION #3 	DO-IT-YOURSELF
While this approach requires more leg work, there is a lot of value in creating real connections
within the Instagram network. There are 4 basic steps involved...
Avoid celebrities with “too good to be true” pricing
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 56 of 62 --

57
There is value in associating with people of higher status.
I’ve elevated my game by paying celebrities to speak at
my events, so there is no shame in paying for shoutouts.
Identify Influencers
While there are search tools designed for specifically finding influencers, they are
costly. You can usually find what you’re looking for by using Instagram’s search
box. Try regular terms and #terms.
1
Engage Their Posts
Always start by providing value and trying to create a relationship. Like your
target influencers posts, leave thoughtful comments and make sure they are a
fit for your brand. It’s not about numbers, it’s about quality.
2
Decide On Strategy
You need to decide if it is best to ask for a reciprocal shout (usually for accounts
similar in size) or if you’re willing to pay them. It doesn’t need to be cash, it could
be a product or some other value.
3
Reachout via DM
Once you’re ready, and ideally you’ve followed each other, shoot them a
message. You should keep it short and sweet, and make sure they see value in
your proposition. Here is a quick example…
4
Hi (their name)! I’ve been following you for a bit and love your stuff,
especially (specific compliment). :)
I’m curious if you ever (swap or do paid) shout outs? I’m only asking
because I think your followers would really like our (posts/products/etc).
Would love to collaborate!
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 57 of 62 --

58
There are many many services and tools out there offering to grow your
followers. It pays to do it right. Avoid any spammy tactics as they can
damage your account.
This trick is one that is actually quite easy to do. While Instagram text is generally the
same standard font, it is possible to customize fonts in some areas.
While this is primarily for fun, having unique lettering can certainly get your posts or
profile to stick out from the crowd. The result could be higher engagements and
potentially even more clicks. Here is how to do it…
When scrolling on Instagram you’ve undoubtedly noticed the blue checkmark by
certain user’s names. This is the official seal that an account is “verified”. This simply
means that Instagram believes the user is someone of status who may be emulated.
They do this to help people steer clear of the copycats.
While this may seem like a small thing, remember that in a scroll-happy world, even
little attention grabbers can help.
#2 USING CUSTOM FONTS
#3 VERIFIED CHECKMARK
Go to a site like lingojam.com or igfonts.io
Type your desired font into the text box
Copy the text of the font style you prefer
Paste the styled text into your comment or bio
1
2
3
4
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 58 of 62 --

59
Getting a verification checkmark is highly sought after in the Instagram world. While
it is not an easy achievement, it can pay big dividends. In addition to the fact that it
provides instant clout, Instagram also gives additional preference to verified accounts.
For example, the first comments listed on a post are always from verified users…
Will the real Grant Cardone please stand up?
Having your comments seen leads to more followers
© 2023 Cardone Training Technologies, Inc. All rights reserved.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 59 of 62 --

60
So how do you get verified? It is a fairly simple process that requires you to apply from
within the app settings. You can get full details in Instagram’s help center
(10xresource.com/ig8).
More importantly though, is what it takes to get approved. Instagram has become fairly
stringent on who they verify, but you can do a few things to improve your odds…
Keep in mind that timing is often critical with this type of thing. For example, if your
company was recently on television or featured in a major publication, you should strike
while the iron is hot. The good news is that you can apply multiple times, so it doesn’t
hurt to try.
Word of Caution: There are many scammers out there who will take your
money with promises of getting your account verified. While there are some
legitimate companies who can assist you (such as odysius.io), you should
always use caution.
Grow your followers (verified accounts generally have 100K or more)
Get press coverage (the more proof you can send of your stature, the better)
Watch google results for your name or company name (they will Google you)
1
2
3
